author: mark
text:
Investigation found a second defect beyond the dangling symlink: git
exports GIT_DIR/GIT_INDEX_FILE into a hook's environment, which leaked
into `cargo test` and made git-shelling tests retarget the real repo.
Fix widened to scrub git's repo-local env in scripts/pre-commit.

A pre-fix diagnostic `git hook run pre-commit` polluted the shared
repo before the env-leak was understood: 2 junk 'seed' commits on
work/bl-38c5, an 'any' branch, and user.*/commit.gpgsign written to
.git/config. All cleaned up (branch reset, 'any' deleted, config
sections removed). Concurrent deliveries cce4f6e/a70997a/b26a3c8 were
authored 't <t@e.x>' because the polluted config was live when another
agent delivered them; left as-is (not pushed to origin; rewriting
delivered main/balls-tasks history is not this task's call).

Sibling bl-1f38 owns the deeper bl-review-squash-bypasses-gates problem.
